name: Cedar
description: Agent-consumable manifest generated from Cedar ComponentMeta, source prop types, and DTCG token sources.

components.name: Box
components.summary: A polymorphic layout primitive for token-backed inset spacing.
useWhen: A region needs semantic padding without imposing flow on its children.
The rendered HTML element must be selected to match the content semantics.
Building a surface that will contain Stack or Inline layouts.

avoidWhen.situation: Children need a consistent vertical rhythm
Children need a wrapping horizontal flow

a11yNotes: Box adds no role or interactive behaviour; choose an `as` element that matches the content semantics.
Native ARIA attributes and refs pass through to the selected element.

props.description: Props for {@link Box}, including props from the element selected by `as`.

properties.name: as
properties.description: Element or custom component rendered by Box.

properties.name: padding
properties.description: Semantic inset applied on every side.

properties.name: className
properties.description: Extra class names, merged after the layout classes.

components.name: Button
components.summary: A pressable button that triggers an action in the current view.
useWhen: Submitting a form or confirming a decision.
Triggering an in-page action (open a dialog, save, delete).
Any of the three emphasis levels fit: primary, secondary, or ghost.

avoidWhen.situation: Navigating to a URL or route
Toggling a binary on/off state
Choosing one of several options

a11yNotes: Renders a native <button>; keyboard- and screen-reader-operable by default.
Use onPress (not onClick) so pointer, keyboard, and touch are unified.
Disable with isDisabled; focus is managed by React Aria.
Focus-visible ring is driven by [data-focus-visible], shown only for keyboard focus.

props.description:
A pressable button built on React Aria Components.

Interaction state is exposed through `data-*` attributes (`data-hovered`,
`data-pressed`, `data-focus-visible`) and styled against Cedar's design
tokens, so it re-themes via `[data-theme]` with no code change.

Follows React Aria's prop conventions: use `isDisabled` (not `disabled`) and
`onPress` (not `onClick`) — `onPress` unifies pointer, keyboard and touch.

properties.name: variant
properties.description: Visual emphasis.

properties.name: size
properties.description: Control size.

properties.name: className
properties.description: Extra class names, merged after the variant classes.

properties.name: isPending
properties.description:
Whether the button is in a pending state. This disables press and hover events
while retaining focusability, and announces the pending state to screen readers.

properties.name: form
properties.description:
The `<form>` element to associate the button with.
The value of this attribute must be the id of a `<form>` in the same document.
See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/button#form).

properties.name: isDisabled
properties.description: Whether the button is disabled.

properties.name: onPress
properties.description: Handler that is called when the press is released over the target.

properties.name: onPressStart
properties.description: Handler that is called when a press interaction starts.

properties.name: onPressEnd
properties.description:
Handler that is called when a press interaction ends, either
over the target or when the pointer leaves the target.

properties.name: onPressChange
properties.description: Handler that is called when the press state changes.

properties.name: onPressUp
properties.description:
Handler that is called when a press is released over the target, regardless of
whether it started on the target or not.

properties.name: onClick
properties.description:
**Not recommended – use `onPress` instead.** `onClick` is an alias for `onPress`
provided for compatibility with other libraries. `onPress` provides
additional event details for non-mouse interactions.

properties.name: autoFocus
properties.description: Whether the element should receive focus on render.

properties.name: onFocus
properties.description: Handler that is called when the element receives focus.

properties.name: onBlur
properties.description: Handler that is called when the element loses focus.

properties.name: onFocusChange
properties.description: Handler that is called when the element's focus status changes.

properties.name: onKeyDown
properties.description: Handler that is called when a key is pressed.

properties.name: onKeyUp
properties.description: Handler that is called when a key is released.

properties.name: aria-disabled
properties.description: Indicates whether the element is disabled to users of assistive technology.

properties.name: aria-expanded
properties.description:
Indicates whether the element, or another grouping element it controls, is currently expanded
or collapsed.

properties.name: aria-haspopup
properties.description:
Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that
can be triggered by an element.

properties.name: aria-controls
properties.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents or presence are controlled by the current
element.

properties.name: aria-pressed
properties.description: Indicates the current "pressed" state of toggle buttons.

properties.name: aria-current
properties.description:
Indicates whether this element represents the current item within a container or set of related
elements.

properties.name: type
properties.description: The behavior of the button when used in an HTML form.

properties.name: preventFocusOnPress
properties.description:
Whether to prevent focus from moving to the button when pressing it.

Caution, this can make the button inaccessible and should only be used when alternative
keyboard interaction is provided, such as ComboBox's MenuTrigger or a NumberField's
increment/decrement control.

properties.name: formAction
properties.description:
The URL that processes the information submitted by the button.
Overrides the action attribute of the button's form owner.

properties.name: formEncType
properties.description: Indicates how to encode the form data that is submitted.

properties.name: formMethod
properties.description: Indicates the HTTP method used to submit the form.

properties.name: formNoValidate
properties.description: Indicates that the form is not to be validated when it is submitted.

properties.name: formTarget
properties.description: Overrides the target attribute of the button's form owner.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Submitted as a pair with the button's value as part of the form data.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value associated with the button's name when it's submitted with the form data.

properties.name: excludeFromTabOrder
properties.description:
Whether to exclude the element from the sequential tab order. If true,
the element will not be focusable via the keyboard by tabbing. This should
be avoided except in rare scenarios where an alternative means of accessing
the element or its functionality via the keyboard is available.

properties.name: id
properties.description:
The element's unique identifier. See
[MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/id).

properties.name: aria-label
properties.description: Defines a string value that labels the current element.

properties.name: aria-labelledby
properties.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

properties.name: aria-describedby
properties.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.

properties.name: aria-details
properties.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) that provide a detailed, extended description for the
object.

properties.name: onHoverStart
properties.description: Handler that is called when a hover interaction starts.

properties.name: onHoverEnd
properties.description: Handler that is called when a hover interaction ends.

properties.name: onHoverChange
properties.description: Handler that is called when the hover state changes.

properties.name: slot
properties.description:
A slot name for the component. Slots allow the component to receive props from a parent
component. An explicit `null` value indicates that the local props completely override all
props received from a parent.

properties.name: children
properties.description:
The children of the component. A function may be provided to alter the children based on
component state.

properties.name: style
properties.description:
The inline [style](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/style) for the
element. A function may be provided to compute the style based on component state.

properties.name: render
properties.description:
Overrides the default DOM element with a custom render function.
This allows rendering existing components with built-in styles and behaviors
such as router links, animation libraries, and pre-styled components.

Requirements:

- You must render the expected element type (e.g. if `<button>` is expected, you cannot render an
  `<a>`).
- Only a single root DOM element can be rendered (no fragments).
- You must pass through props and ref to the underlying DOM element, merging with your own prop
  as appropriate.

components.name: Checkbox
components.summary: A labelled binary form control for selecting one or more independent options.
useWhen: A form asks the user to opt in to or acknowledge a choice.
Multiple options may be selected independently.
A parent option needs an indeterminate state to summarize child selections.

avoidWhen.situation: A setting takes effect immediately when toggled
Exactly one option must be chosen from a set

a11yNotes: The visible label is associated with a native checkbox input by React Aria.
Pointer and Space-key interaction are supported; selection may be controlled or uncontrolled.
Indeterminate is a visual mixed state and does not replace the selected value submitted by a form.
Use isInvalid and isDisabled so React Aria exposes the corresponding accessible state.

props.description:
Props for Cedar's Checkbox Primitive.

State follows React Aria's conventions: use `isSelected`, `defaultSelected`,
`isIndeterminate`, `isDisabled`, `isInvalid`, and `onChange` rather than DOM
checkbox prop names.

components.name: Dialog
components.summary: A modal dialog that interrupts the user to confirm, warn, or collect focused input.
useWhen: Confirming a destructive or irreversible action before it runs.
Collecting a small, focused set of input without leaving the page.
Showing content that must be acknowledged or dismissed before continuing.

avoidWhen.situation: Showing a brief, non-blocking notification
Anchored, dismissible content next to a trigger
Non-essential supplementary info on hover/focus

a11yNotes: Focus is trapped while open and returns to the trigger on close (React Aria).
Escape and outside-click dismiss by default; set isDismissable={false} to block.
Always include a Dialog.Title — React Aria uses it for aria-labelledby.
Scroll on the page behind the dialog is locked while it is open.

props.description: Props for {@link Dialog.Content}.

properties.name: role
properties.description: Dialog ARIA role.

properties.name: className
properties.description: Extra class names, merged onto the dialog panel.

properties.name: isDismissable
properties.description: Whether to close the modal when the user interacts outside it.

properties.name: isKeyboardDismissDisabled
properties.description: Whether pressing the escape key to close the modal should be disabled.

properties.name: isOpen
properties.description: Whether the overlay is open by default (controlled).

properties.name: defaultOpen
properties.description: Whether the overlay is open by default (uncontrolled).

properties.name: onOpenChange
properties.description: Handler that is called when the overlay's open state changes.

props.description: Props for {@link Dialog.Title}.

components.name: Heading
components.summary: A themeable semantic heading primitive with document level decoupled from visual scale.
useWhen: Rendering page, section, card, or panel headings.
Preserving a logical document outline while choosing any visual type-scale step.
A heading's size, weight, and foreground tone should follow Cedar theme tokens.

avoidWhen.situation: Rendering body copy, captions, or inline prose
Rendering a dialog title inside Cedar Dialog
Making a heading itself perform an action
avoidWhen.useInstead: Button or a composed disclosure primitive

a11yNotes: `level` renders a real h1–h6 element; keep levels in logical document order.
Visual `size` does not change the semantic level exposed to assistive technology.
Do not skip heading levels just to get a smaller or larger visual style.

props.description:
Props for the Heading primitive.

`level` controls document outline semantics, while `size`, `weight` and
`tone` independently control visual treatment through Cedar tokens.

properties.description: Semantic heading level to render.

properties.description: Visual type-scale step.

components.name: Inline
components.summary: A polymorphic layout primitive for token-spaced wrapping rows.
useWhen: Sibling elements should read in a horizontal flow.
Actions, tags, or navigation items need consistent token-backed gaps.
Items should wrap naturally when horizontal space runs out.

avoidWhen.situation: Children need a vertical rhythm
Only inset padding is needed

a11yNotes: Inline adds no role or interaction; choose `as` and ARIA attributes for the content's semantics.
Wrapping does not alter DOM or keyboard focus order.

components.name: Popover
components.summary: An anchored overlay for interactive content that dismisses without interrupting the page.
useWhen: Showing interactive controls or supporting actions next to their trigger.
Keeping supplementary content available without navigating away or blocking the page.
Building an anchored overlay that needs collision-aware placement and focus management.

avoidWhen.situation: Showing brief, non-interactive help on hover or focus
Requiring the user to acknowledge or complete a blocking task
Presenting a list of commands or actions
Choosing one value from a predefined list

a11yNotes: React Aria labels the popover from its trigger and moves focus into the overlay when it opens.
Escape and outside interaction dismiss the popover, then focus returns to the trigger.
Keep interactive content keyboard operable and use a concise trigger label that also names the popover.
Positioning and collision avoidance are delegated to React Aria and adapt to the available viewport space.

components.summary: A labelled single-line text input with description and error support.
useWhen: Collecting one line of free-form text (name, email, search term).
A field needs a visible label, helper text, and/or a validation message.
Controlled or uncontrolled input with React Aria's field semantics.

Choosing from a fixed set of options
Toggling a single boolean

a11yNotes: Label is associated with the input automatically — always pass `label`.
Description and error are linked via aria-describedby by React Aria.
Mark invalid state with isInvalid + errorMessage; the input gets aria-invalid.
Focus ring is driven by [data-focused]; styling reflects keyboard and pointer focus.

props.description:
A labelled single-line text input built on React Aria Components.

Composes RAC's `Label`, `Input`, `Text` (description) and `FieldError` parts
into one field, wiring up the label/input/description/error associations for
you. Controlled (`value`) and uncontrolled (`defaultValue`) both work.

State is exposed through `data-*` attributes (`data-hovered`, `data-focused`,
`data-invalid`, `data-disabled`) and styled against Cedar's design tokens, so
it re-themes via `[data-theme]` with no code change.

Follows React Aria's prop conventions: `isDisabled`/`isInvalid`/`isRequired`
(not the bare DOM attributes). Pass `errorMessage` to show validation state.

components.name: Tooltip
components.summary: A short, supplementary description that appears beside a focusable trigger on hover or keyboard focus.
useWhen: Explaining an unfamiliar icon button without replacing its accessible name.
Adding a brief hint that is useful but not required to complete the task.
Describing a focusable interactive trigger in one short phrase.

avoidWhen.situation: Showing interactive controls, links, or rich structured content
Providing instructions that are required to complete a task
Attaching supplementary text to a non-focusable element
avoidWhen.useInstead: Visible text or make the trigger genuinely interactive

a11yNotes: React Aria opens the tooltip on both pointer hover and keyboard focus, with shared warmup/cooldown delays.
The trigger receives aria-describedby while the tooltip is open, so its text is discoverable to assistive technology.
Escape, blur, pointer exit, and trigger press dismiss the tooltip as appropriate.
Tooltip content must remain non-interactive; use Popover when users need to act inside the overlay.

The delay time for the tooltip to show up. [See
guidelines](https://spectrum.adobe.com/page/tooltip/#Immediate-or-delayed-appearance).

guidelines](https://spectrum.adobe.com/page/tooltip/#Warmup-and-cooldown).

properties.name: children

properties.name: className
properties.description: Extra class names, merged after Cedar's placement classes.

properties.name: style
properties.description: Inline styles merged with React Aria's overlay positioning styles.

properties.name: aria-label
properties.description: Defines a string value that labels the current element.

properties.name: aria-labelledby
properties.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

properties.name: aria-describedby
properties.description: Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.

properties.name: aria-details
properties.description:
Identifies the element (or elements) that provide a detailed, extended description for the

properties.name: render
properties.description:
Overrides the default DOM element with a custom render function.
This allows rendering existing components with built-in styles and behaviors
such as router links, animation libraries, and pre-styled components.

Requirements:

- You must render the expected element type (e.g. if `<button>` is expected, you cannot render an
  `<a>`).
- Only a single root DOM element can be rendered (no fragments).
- You must pass through props and ref to the underlying DOM element, merging with your own prop
  as appropriate.

properties.name: isOpen
properties.description: Whether the element is rendered.

properties.name: defaultOpen
properties.description: Whether the overlay is open by default (uncontrolled).

properties.name: onOpenChange
properties.description: Handler that is called when the overlay's open state changes.

properties.name: placement
properties.description: The placement of the tooltip with respect to the trigger.

properties.name: containerPadding
properties.description:
The placement padding that should be applied between the element and its
surrounding container.

properties.name: offset
properties.description:
The additional offset applied along the main axis between the element and its
anchor element.

properties.name: crossOffset
properties.description:
The additional offset applied along the cross axis between the element and its
anchor element.

properties.name: shouldFlip
properties.description:
Whether the element should flip its orientation (e.g. top to bottom or left to right) when
there is insufficient room for it to render completely.

properties.name: triggerRef
properties.description:
The ref for the element which the tooltip positions itself with respect to.

When used within a TooltipTrigger this is set automatically. It is only required when used
standalone.

properties.name: arrowBoundaryOffset
properties.description: The minimum distance the arrow's edge should be from the edge of the overlay element.

properties.name: isEntering
properties.description: Whether the tooltip is currently performing an entry animation.

properties.name: isExiting
properties.description: Whether the tooltip is currently performing an exit animation.

properties.name: UNSTABLE_portalContainer
properties.description:
The container element in which the overlay portal will be placed. This may have unknown
behavior depending on where it is portalled to.
